methodsAttempted.query: site:vu.lt Vilnius University generative AI artificial intelligence policy students teachers
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search initially returned mostly faculty news, journal PDFs, and study-program pages rather than central policy evidence.

methodsAttempted.query: site:vu.lt Vilniaus universitetas dirbtinis intelektas politika studentams dėstytojams ChatGPT
methodsAttempted.notes: Lithuanian Firecrawl search exposed official faculty/event pages and helped confirm source-language terms for AI and generative AI.

methodsAttempted.query: site:vu.lt/en/studies academic information students regulations artificial intelligence Vilnius University
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the Faculty of Communication study-documents page, which links official Vilnius University AI usage guidelines and faculty-level AI documents.

methodsAttempted.query: Regulations and Documents The Guidelines on Artificial Intelligence Usage at Vilnius University
methodsAttempted.notes: The official Vilnius University regulations index lists the AI usage guidelines under Study Regulations and Academic Policies.

methodsAttempted.query: Dirbtinio intelekto naudojimo Vilniaus universitete gairės pdf
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the Lithuanian source PDF, the official English translation, and faculty documents that cite the Senate-approved central guidelines.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found Senate-approved Vilnius University AI usage guidelines in Lithuanian, an official regulations index linking the guidelines, and two faculty-level implementation documents from the Faculty of Communication and Vilnius University Business School. News, training, and study-program pages were rejected for policy extraction because they do not establish binding university-wide policy.

artifacts.claimText: Vilnius University has a Senate-approved central AI usage guideline listed among its official study regulations and academic policy documents.

citation.citationTitle: Dirbtinio intelekto naudojimo Vilniaus universitete gairės

artifacts.claimText: Vilnius University's AI guidelines state that use of generative AI models in research, written work, applications, and similar work must be clearly indicated.

citation.citationTitle: Dirbtinio intelekto naudojimo Vilniaus universitete gairės

artifacts.claimText: The Vilnius University AI guidelines treat undisclosed use of a generative AI model in academic work as academic dishonesty.

citation.citationTitle: Dirbtinio intelekto naudojimo Vilniaus universitete gairės

artifacts.claimText: Vilnius University's AI guidelines say confidential information, personal data, sensitive information, and unpublished research data must not be uploaded to generative AI models.

citation.citationTitle: Dirbtinio intekto naudojimo Vilniaus universitete gairės

artifacts.claimText: Vilnius University's AI guidelines state that lecturers should not use generative AI models to prepare reviews of final theses.

citation.citationTitle: Dirbtinio intelekto naudojimo Vilniaus universitete gairės

artifacts.claimText: Within the Vilnius University Faculty of Communication, a declaration on the use of artificial intelligence tools must accompany all written academic works submitted within the Faculty.

citation.citationTitle: VU Faculty of Communication - Documents regulating studies

artifacts.claimText: Vilnius University Faculty of Communication recommendations allow AI use in assignments as specified by teaching staff, require acknowledgment, and set a 5% character-count limit for AI-generated text in assessed written assignments.

citation.citationTitle: Recommendations on the Use of Artificial Intelligence in Study Assignments at the Faculty of Communication, Vilnius University

artifacts.claimText: Vilnius University Business School guidelines allow students to use AI tools in academic works, while requiring disclosure and treating failure to specify AI use and provide a transcript in annexes as academic dishonesty.

citation.citationTitle: Vilnius University Business School Guidelines for the Ethical Use of Artificial Intelligence in Academic Work

artifacts.claimText: Vilnius University Business School guidelines say public or free AI tools must not receive research data, study materials, work drafts, notes, or personal information, and Level 2-5 data must not be entered unless approved by the University and explicitly permitted.

citation.citationTitle: Vilnius University Business School Guidelines for the Ethical Use of Artificial Intelligence in Academic Work

artifacts.sourceTitle: Vilnius University Business School Guidelines for the Ethical Use of Artificial Intelligence in Academic Work
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Students must not upload research data, study materials, work drafts, notes, or personal information. Only Level 1 data may be shared with publicly accessible AI tools without prior permission. Level 2–5 data must never be entered or shared with AI tools ... unless the AI tool has been approved by Vilnius University and the student has received explicit permission.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Data Classification Levels for Student Work and Research

citation.citationTitle: Vilnius University Business School Guidelines for the Ethical Use of Artificial Intelligence in Academic Work

artifacts.notes: Approved as a staged source-status candidate because both the Lithuanian guideline PDF and official regulations index support the existence and source status of central AI guidance.
Approved with narrow wording that follows the source's explicit disclosure requirement.
Approved because the Lithuanian text directly says undisclosed generative AI use in academic work is academic dishonesty.
Approved with direct support from the Data Security section; no broader approved-tool claim is inferred.
Approved as a teaching/process claim scoped to lecturers and final-thesis review preparation.
Approved only as a Faculty of Communication claim; not treated as university-wide.
Approved only as Faculty of Communication guidance because the PDF title and text are faculty-scoped.
Approved only as a Vilnius University Business School claim; not treated as a central university-wide rule.
Approved only as Business School guidance; the broader university-level privacy claim is separately supported by the central Lithuanian guidelines.

artifacts.title: Vilnius University AI Policy Staged Review
artifacts.summary: Staged review found Senate-approved Vilnius University AI usage guidelines in Lithuanian, linked from an official regulations index, plus faculty-scoped implementation documents from the Faculty of Communication and Vilnius University Business School. Claims are limited to the official text: central guidance on disclosure, academic dishonesty, data security, and thesis-review use, with separate scoped faculty claims where the source is faculty-specific.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Faculty of Communication and Vilnius University Business School claims are scoped to those units and are not treated as university-wide rules.
News, events, training pages, and study-program marketing pages were rejected as policy evidence.
